issues.title: Type mismatch in hostflag range check and pointer cast
issues.problem: The code casts input to int64_t* for range checking but passes the same pointer to a function expecting uint32_t*. The comparison *(int64_t *)input > UINT32_MAX will never be true for negative values since they're cast as signed. More critically, passing (int64_t *)input to X509_SetHostFlags which expects uint32_t* is incorrect - on little-endian systems this happens to work, but it's fundamentally wrong and could fail on other architectures or with different compiler optimizations.

issues.title: DNS/CN matching no longer validates hostnameLen equals strlen(hostname)
issues.problem: The validation `if (hostnameLen != (uint32_t)strlen(hostname))` was removed. This means: 1) Hostnames with embedded NUL bytes can bypass validation (e.g., "evil.com\0" with hostnameLen=4 would pass as "evil"), 2) The function still treats hostname as a C string in the CN fallback path via X509_VerifyHostnameWithCn which uses string semantics, 3) Binary IP addresses can fall through to CN matching when SAN is absent, treating binary data as a C string.

issues.title: Connection-specific host state stored in shared store context
issues.problem: The fields hostnames, ip, ipLen, hostflags, and peername were added to HITLS_X509_VerifyParam which lives inside HITLS_X509_StoreCtx. Store contexts are refcount-shared when one HITLS_Config is cloned into multiple HITLS_Ctx objects. This means sibling connections created from the same config will overwrite each other's verification targets and peer names. Connection-specific state should be on the per-connection object, not in the shared store.

issues.title: SetVerifyParam can return success with stale/missing host targets after allocation failure
issues.problem: X509_SetVerifyParam frees the old hostname list before copying the new one, never checks if BSL_LIST_Copy() failed, and leaves the previous host/IP untouched when the incoming field is NULL. After an allocation failure, the hostnames list is freed but not replaced, silently disabling hostname verification. If verifyParam->hostnames is NULL, the old list remains intact, which may prevent intended clearing or replacement.

issues.title: Peername field not cleared on verification failure or IP-only match
issues.problem: The peername field is only updated inside the successful DNS hostname verification branch in CheckHostnames. If a previous verification matched a hostname and the next verification: 1) fails completely, or 2) only matches via IP SAN (not DNS hostname), the peername retains the old value from the previous connection. HITLS_GetPeerName() would return stale connection data.

issues.title: Incorrect memcpy_s size parameter in X509_SetVerifyHost
issues.problem: The memcpy_s call uses hostnameLen as the size parameter, but the buffer was allocated with hostnameLen + 1 bytes. While the code then sets tmp[hostnameLen] = '\0' separately, using hostnameLen as the memcpy_s size means it won't copy the null terminator even if the source has one. This is technically correct but inconsistent with the allocation pattern.

issues.title: Unnecessary null byte appended to binary IP address storage
issues.problem: The function allocates ipLen + 1 bytes and appends a null terminator. IP addresses are binary data, not null-terminated strings, and ipLen is tracked separately. The extra byte is wasteful and the null terminator serves no purpose for binary data.

issues.title: Redundant null terminator assignment in DupString
issues.problem: BSL_SAL_Dump is called with strlen(str) + 1 which should include the null terminator. Then the code explicitly sets the null terminator again at the same position, which is redundant.

issues.title: Redundant null terminator for IP address in X509_SetVerifyParam
issues.problem: The IP buffer is allocated with ipLen + 1 bytes and a null terminator is set. IP addresses are binary data and don't need null termination since ipLen tracks the actual length. The extra byte is wasted.

issues.title: Redundant null terminator assignment in CheckHostnames
issues.problem: After allocating with calloc (which zero-initializes) and copying with memcpy_s, the null terminator is set redundantly. The code can be simplified to copy both data and null terminator in a single memcpy_s call.
